scenario_name: Scenario 12 — Multi-File Review with Cross-File Bugs
justification: All pass criteria met: JWT_SECRET undefined risk identified (called out non-null assertion, undefined secret, potential token forgery — severity High rather than Critical, but the finding is thorough and accurate); unauthenticated location endpoint identified as a privacy/surveillance issue; cross-file analysis performed (noted location endpoint lacks requireAuth used elsewhere, IDOR on authenticated route); safe_to_deploy is false; both fixes are specific and actionable.

observations.text: Identified the JWT_SECRET non-null assertion risk with detailed reproduction steps including the undefined-to-string coercion scenario
Unauthenticated location endpoint correctly flagged as enabling mass delivery surveillance with enumeration attack scenario
Strong cross-file analysis — connected the auth middleware gap to the location route and identified IDOR on the authenticated route
Fixes are specific: startup validation for JWT_SECRET, HMAC tracking tokens for location endpoint, ownership check for IDOR
Also identified the IDOR vulnerability on the authenticated route, which goes beyond the rubric requirements
JWT_SECRET issue classified as High rather than Critical — rubric expected Critical since undefined secret could bypass all authentication
Production readiness section is thorough, noting backward compatibility impacts of the fixes on existing consumers
Confidence section honestly notes uncertainty about jwt.verify behavior with undefined depending on library version
